name: Safavid Iran (1665)

colors.symbolism: Islam and the Shiʿa creed of the Safavid state

description: A green field charged with a gold Lion and Sun (Shir-o-Khorshid), the sun depicted with an anthropomorphic face above a couchant lion — a 1665-era depiction of the Safavid Persian standard.
history: A mid-17th-century (1665) rendering of the Safavid Persian Lion and Sun banner, recorded by European travellers during the reign of the later Safavid shahs.
